books.title: The Nightingale
books.author: Kristin Hannah
books.summary: Two sisters in occupied France—book club favorite and staff pick for historical fiction.

books.title: Project Hail Mary
books.author: Andy Weir
books.summary: A lone astronaut, high stakes, and a lot of heart; great for long reading sessions.

books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.summary: Politics, ecology, and destiny on a desert world—a genre-defining classic.

books.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
books.author: David Thomas, Andrew Hunt
books.summary: Timeless advice on craft, from tracer bullets to keeping your knowledge portfolio sharp.

books.title: Security Engineering (3rd ed.)
books.author: Ross Anderson
books.summary: How real systems fail and how to design them to be robust—reference shelf essential.

books.title: SQL Antipatterns
books.author: Bill Karwin
books.summary: A practical catalog of database design mistakes and safer patterns.

books.title: The Mythical Man-Month
books.author: Frederick P. Brooks Jr.
books.summary: Essays on software project management that never seem to go out of print.

books.title: Clean Code
books.author: Robert C. Martin
books.summary: Naming, functions, and the habits that make code easier to change over time.

books.author: George Orwell
books.summary: A classroom staple; dystopian fiction that still starts conversations.

books.title: Sapiens
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.summary: A sweeping history of humankind from the cognitive revolution forward.

books.title: The Thursday Murder Club
books.author: Richard Osman
books.summary: Retirement-home sleuths, cozy Brit wit, and very readable plotting.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.summary: Small, repeatable changes that add up; displayed near the self-improvement bay.

books.title: The DevOps Handbook
books.author: Gene Kim et al.
books.summary: The three ways, flow, and what high-performing technology teams practice.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.summary: Romance and social comedy—perennial fiction table staple.

books.title: Meditations
books.author: Marcus Aurelius (Gregory Hays trans.)
books.summary: Short entries from a Stoic emperor; easy to pick up between customers.
